title: Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil

seo.title: Authentic Rosemary Focaccia Genovese Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master authentic Rosemary Focaccia Genovese with our easy recipe. Learn to bake this traditional Italian bread with crispy crust, soft interior, and fragrant olive oil.

best Rosemary Focaccia Genovese at home

seo.imageAlt: Close-up of freshly baked Rosemary Focaccia Genovese with olive oil dimples and sea salt
seo.imageCaption: Savor the aroma and taste of homemade Rosemary Focaccia Genovese, a classic Italian bread perfect for any meal!

description: Imagine sinking your teeth into a perfectly baked Focaccia Genovese, its golden crust glistening with fragrant olive oil and sprinkled with fresh rosemary and sea salt. This traditional Ligurian bread boasts a wonderfully airy, soft interior with those signature dimples, making it an irresistible accompaniment to any Italian meal or a delightful snack on its own. Its simplicity and rich flavor epitomize the heart of Italian baking.

ingredients.notes: plus extra for drizzling and pan

instructions.title: Activate the Yeast
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the warm water and yeast. Let it sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy, indicating the yeast is active. This step is crucial for a light and airy focaccia.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: Add the flour, fine sea salt, and 2 tablespoons (30ml) of olive oil to the yeast mixture. Mix with a wooden spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms. Transfer the dough to a lightly floured surface and knead for 8-10 minutes until it is smooth and elastic. The dough should be soft and slightly sticky.

instructions.title: First Rise
instructions.text: Lightly oil a clean bowl with olive oil. Place the dough in the bowl, turning it once to coat. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap or a damp kitchen towel and let it rise in a warm place for 1 to 1.5 hours, or until it has doubled in size. This slow rise develops flavor.

instructions.title: Shape the Focaccia
instructions.text: Generously drizzle a 9x13 inch baking pan with olive oil. Gently transfer the risen dough to the prepared pan. Using your fingertips, gently stretch and press the dough to fill the pan, being careful not to deflate it too much. Create characteristic dimples all over the surface of the dough with your fingers.

instructions.title: Second Rise and Topping
instructions.text: Drizzle another 2 tablespoons (30ml) of olive oil over the dimpled dough. Sprinkle generously with chopped fresh rosemary and flaky sea salt. Cover the pan loosely with plastic wrap and let it rise again in a warm place for 30-45 minutes, or until visibly puffy.

instructions.title: Bake to Golden Perfection
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 220C (425F). Bake the focaccia for 20-25 minutes, or until it is golden brown on top and cooked through. The crust should be crisp and the interior soft. For extra crispiness, you can lightly mist the oven with water at the beginning of baking.

instructions.title: Cool and Serve
instructions.text: Once baked, immediately transfer the focaccia from the pan to a wire rack to cool slightly. Drizzle with a final touch of extra virgin olive oil if desired. Serve warm or at room temperature, cut into squares or strips. It is best enjoyed fresh from the oven.

tips: For the most authentic Focaccia Genovese, use high-quality extra virgin olive oil, as its flavor is central to the dish. Drizzle it generously!
Do not overwork the dough during the second rise or when dimpling; gentle handling ensures an airy texture. The more dimples, the better the olive oil pockets.
Focaccia is best enjoyed the day it's made. If you have leftovers, refresh them in a warm oven for a few minutes to regain some crispness.
article:
Focaccia Genovese. Just hearing the name conjures images of sun-drenched Ligurian coastlines, the salty breeze, and the irresistible aroma of freshly baked bread. This isn't just any flatbread; it's a culinary masterpiece, a testament to the simplicity and profound deliciousness that Italian baking offers. With its impossibly light and airy interior, crisp golden crust, and a generous anointing of robust olive oil and fragrant rosemary, the authentic Focaccia Genovese is more than a dish – it's an experience. Today, we're diving into the world of authentic Rosemary Focaccia Genovese, a staple that transforms simple ingredients into something truly extraordinary. Whether you’re an experienced baker or a curious beginner, mastering this recipe will bring a slice of Italian tradition right into your kitchen.

## What is Focaccia Genovese?

At its heart, Focaccia Genovese is a traditional Ligurian flatbread, hailing from the bustling port city of Genoa. Unlike many other variations of focaccia found across Italy, the Genovese style is distinctively thin, often boasting a delicate, almost cracker-like crust, while maintaining a wonderfully soft and chewy crumb within. Its surface is famously dotted with "fossa" or dimples, which are not merely decorative but serve a crucial purpose: they act as small wells to hold puddles of the best extra virgin olive oil and flaky sea salt, ensuring every bite is bursting with flavor and moisture.

The history of focaccia stretches back to ancient times, with its name deriving from the Latin "panis focacius," meaning "bread baked on the hearth." Over centuries, it evolved into the beloved staple it is today, deeply embedded in Ligurian culture. It’s often enjoyed for breakfast, dipped in coffee or milk, or as a snack throughout the day. The classic Rosemary Focaccia Genovese is distinguished by its golden hue, the irresistible aroma of fresh rosemary, and the generous use of high-quality extra virgin olive oil, which is arguably its most defining characteristic. It’s a celebration of minimal ingredients yielding maximum flavor, proving that sometimes, less truly is more.

## Ingredients You Need for Rosemary Focaccia Genovese

Crafting the perfect Rosemary Focaccia Genovese relies on a few key, high-quality ingredients. Don't skimp, especially on the olive oil – it's the soul of this bread!

*   **Bread Flour (Strong Flour):** 500g (about 4 cups). High protein content is essential for a good chew.
*   **Warm Water:** 350ml (about 1 ½ cups), ideally around 105-115°F (40-46°C).
*   **Active Dry Yeast:** 7g (about 2 ¼ teaspoons or one standard packet).
*   **Granulated Sugar:** 5g (about 1 teaspoon). Helps activate the yeast.
*   **Fine Sea Salt:** 10g (about 2 teaspoons) for the dough.
*   **Extra Virgin Olive Oil:** At least 100ml (about ½ cup), plus extra for the pan and topping. Choose a good quality, fruity oil.
*   **Fresh Rosemary:** 2-3 sprigs, finely chopped, for topping.
*   **Flaky Sea Salt:** For sprinkling on top before baking.

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Baking

Follow these steps carefully to achieve an authentic and delicious Rosemary Focaccia Genovese.

1.  **Activate the Yeast:** In a small bowl, combine the warm water and sugar. Sprinkle the active dry yeast over the top and let it sit for 5-10 minutes until it becomes foamy. This indicates the yeast is active.
2.  **Prepare the Dough:** In a large mixing bowl, combine the bread flour and fine sea salt. Pour in the activated yeast mixture and 50ml (¼ cup) of the extra virgin olive oil. Mix with a wooden spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms.
3.  **Knead (Lightly):** Transfer the dough to a lightly floured surface. Knead for about 5-7 minutes until the dough is smooth and elastic. It will be a relatively sticky dough – embrace it!
4.  **First Rise:** Lightly oil a clean bowl with olive oil. Place the dough in the bowl, turning it once to coat. Cover the bowl tightly with plastic wrap or a clean kitchen towel. Let it rise in a warm place for 1.5 to 2 hours, or until it has doubled in size.
5.  **Prepare the Baking Pan:** Generously grease a 9x13 inch (23x33 cm) baking pan with at least 2-3 tablespoons of extra virgin olive oil. Ensure the entire bottom and sides are well coated. This prevents sticking and contributes to the crispy crust.
6.  **Transfer and Stretch:** Gently transfer the risen dough into the prepared baking pan. Using your oiled fingertips, gently press and stretch the dough to fill the pan. If the dough resists and springs back, cover it with plastic wrap and let it rest for 10-15 minutes before continuing to stretch. This allows the gluten to relax.
7.  **Second Rise:** Once the dough roughly fills the pan, cover it again with plastic wrap and let it rise in a warm place for another 30-45 minutes, or until visibly puffy.
8.  **Dimple and Top:** Preheat your oven to 425°F (220°C). Once the dough has completed its second rise, use your oiled fingertips to create deep indentations all over the surface of the dough – these are your "fossa." Don't be shy; press firmly but gently. Drizzle the remaining 50ml (¼ cup) of extra virgin olive oil generously over the dimpled surface. Sprinkle with the chopped fresh rosemary and flaky sea salt.
9.  **Bake:** Place the pan in the preheated oven and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the focaccia is golden brown on top and the bottom is crisp.
10. **Cool:** Once baked, immediately transfer the Rosemary Focaccia Genovese to a wire rack to cool slightly. Serve warm or at room temperature.

## Pro Tips for the Best Focaccia Genovese

Achieving that perfect, authentic Focaccia Genovese texture and flavor can be elevated with a few expert insights.

*   **Embrace the Stickiness:** Focaccia dough is a high-hydration dough, meaning it will be quite sticky. Don't be tempted to add too much flour during kneading or handling, as this will result in a dry, dense focaccia. Oiling your hands and the work surface is key.
*   **Quality Olive Oil is Non-Negotiable:** Seriously, this is where the magic happens. A good quality extra virgin olive oil provides flavor, moisture, and that signature crispy crust. Don't hold back when oiling the pan and topping the dough.
*   **Deep Dimples:** The "fossa" (dimples) are not just for show. They create little pockets that hold the olive oil and salt, infusing every bite with incredible flavor and preventing the dough from puffing up too much like a regular bread loaf. Ensure they are deep and distinct.
*   **Patience with Rising:** Don't rush the rising process. Proper fermentation develops flavor and creates that characteristic airy crumb. A warm, draft-free environment is ideal.
*   **Hot Oven, Quick Bake:** Focaccia bakes best in a hot oven. This helps create a beautiful golden crust quickly while keeping the inside tender.
*   **Listen to Your Dough:** If the dough springs back too much when you're trying to stretch it in the pan, cover it and let it rest for 10-15 minutes. This allows the gluten to relax, making it easier to work with. Mastering the art of Rosemary Focaccia Genovese involves a few key techniques, but the most important is to enjoy the process!

## Serving Suggestions for Your Focaccia

A freshly baked Rosemary Focaccia Genovese is incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in countless ways, from a simple snack to a sophisticated accompaniment.

*   **As is:** Often, the best way to enjoy it is simply on its own, perhaps with an extra drizzle of good olive oil. It's fantastic for breakfast with a cup of coffee, just like they do in Liguria.
*   **Dipping:** Serve with a small bowl of high-quality extra virgin olive oil, balsamic vinegar, or a pesto dip.
*   **Charcuterie and Cheese:** It makes an excellent addition to a cheese board or charcuterie platter, pairing beautifully with cured meats, olives, and various cheeses.
*   **Soups and Salads:** Tear off pieces to sop up the delicious broths of minestrone, tomato soup, or to accompany a fresh garden salad.
*   **Sandwiches:** Slice it horizontally and fill it with your favorite sandwich ingredients – prosciutto, mozzarella, roasted vegetables, or a simple tomato and basil.
*   **Appetizer:** Cut into small squares and serve as an elegant appetizer at your next gathering.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Rosemary Focaccia Genovese is perfect as is, there's always room for creative twists once you've mastered the basic recipe.

*   **Other Herbs:** Experiment with different fresh herbs. Thyme, oregano, or a mix of Italian herbs can be wonderful alternatives or additions to rosemary.
*   **Savory Toppings:** Before the final rise and dimpling, press in small cherry tomatoes (halved), thinly sliced red onion, black olives, or even thinly sliced garlic for added flavor.
*   **Flavored Oils:** Infuse your olive oil with garlic cloves, chili flakes, or lemon zest for a subtle flavor boost before drizzling it over the dough.
*   **Whole Wheat Flour:** For a nuttier flavor and a slightly denser crumb, substitute up to 100g of the bread flour with whole wheat flour.
*   **Gluten-Free:** While not traditional, gluten-free bread flour blends can be used for those with dietary restrictions. Be aware that the texture will differ slightly.
*   **Sweet Focaccia:** For a departure from savory, you can even make a sweet version by sprinkling sugar and grapes or thinly sliced apples before baking.

Baking your own authentic Focaccia Genovese is a rewarding journey into the heart of Italian baking. The aroma that fills your home, the simple act of dimpling the dough, and the ultimate joy of biting into a warm, savory slice are truly unmatched. So, gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to delight your senses with the incredible flavors of this classic Italian flatbread. Embrace the joy of baking and savor every bite of your homemade Rosemary Focaccia Genovese!
